title: The Banana Book
body: Why did the banana bring a book to breakfast? It wanted something good to peel over while everyone ate. The pages were a little slippery, but the jokes were very ripe.

title: The Pocket Cloud
body: Mina found a tiny cloud that fit inside her coat pocket. It did not rain. It only puffed out cool air when someone looked sleepy. By lunchtime, everyone wanted a turn carrying the polite little cloud.

title: The Fast Turtle
body: What did the turtle say when it finished its homework early? Shell yes. Then it took a victory lap so slow that it was still celebrating at snack time.

title: One Bright Marble
body: Owen kept a bright blue marble in his pocket for brave days. Before reading aloud, he touched it once and took one slow breath. The words came out steady, and his smile stayed all the way home.

title: Library Lion
body: Why was the lion so quiet in the library? It did not want to use its inside roar. It whispered for a joke book instead and left with three.

title: The Helpful Hat
body: Nora's red hat had one excellent talent. Whenever someone forgot something, the hat tipped itself toward the missing item. Gloves, pencils, lunchbox, all found. The hat never bragged about it once.

title: A Snowman's Snack
body: What is a snowman's favorite breakfast? Frosted flakes, of course. The bowl has to be extra cold, or the chef gets suspicious.

title: The Tiny Parade
body: After the rain stopped, Leo noticed three ants carrying a petal like a parade float. He saluted them from the path. The ants did not wave back, but they marched with excellent style.
